ledger_scope: Normalized source-boundary contract for Big Five Result Page V2 asset-agent planning and future candidate constraints.
negative_guarantees: No selector assets are generated by this ledger.
No content assets are generated by this ledger.
No final big5_result_page_v2 payload is generated by this ledger.
No fap-web result-page copy is created or modified.
No CMS import, release snapshot, runtime wrapper, pilot gate, production import gate, rollout gate, sitemap, llms, or search queue behavior is changed.

No BFI-2 item text, BFI-2 scoring text, proprietary report prose, competitor copy, private score payload, raw score, vector, percentile, attempt id, editor notes, QA notes, or internal metadata is copied into public payloads.

sources.title: International Personality Item Pool official site
permitted_use: Use as public-domain Big Five/IPIP measurement context.
Use as source-boundary evidence for IPIP-derived measurement context in future candidate planning.
Use only with local selector, safety, claim, route, and rendered-preview QA before any promotion.
disallowed_use: Do not treat IPIP as proof of FermatMind private percentile, norm eligibility, score interpretation, or production readiness.
Do not expose raw item responses, raw scores, domain vectors, facet vectors, percentiles, attempt ids, private URLs, or internal metadata.
Do not use IPIP to justify deterministic type labels, diagnosis, hiring screening, treatment, or success prediction claims.

copy_policy.notes: IPIP source material may be public-domain context, but FermatMind public payloads must still be locally authored, reviewed, and validated.

claim_trace_requirements: Claims must cite this source_id and include a method-boundary limitation.
Claims must not imply FermatMind norm eligibility, percentile availability, or runtime readiness.

sources.title: The Big Five Inventory-2 (BFI-2), Colby Personality Lab
permitted_use: Use as high-level structure reference for Big Five domain and facet framing.
Use as terminology and method-boundary reference only.
Use to keep future facet claims weaker than independent measurement unless local support fields prove otherwise.
disallowed_use: Do not copy BFI-2 item text.
Do not copy BFI-2 scoring text.
Do not copy BFI-2 report copy or body prose.
Do not represent FermatMind assets as official BFI-2 reports.
Do not use BFI-2 as support for hiring, diagnosis, therapy, treatment, or success prediction claims.

copy_policy.notes: BFI-2 is structure and terminology reference only; no item text, scoring text, report prose, or body prose may be copied.

claim_trace_requirements: Facet-related claims must include the structure-only limitation.
Any generated facet candidate must pass selector contract, safety scan, and independent-measurement checks before review.

sources.title: Alheimsins bigfive-web GitHub repository
permitted_use: Use as open-source implementation reference for non-copy architecture comparisons.
Use to compare separation of test flow, scoring flow, and result rendering at a structural level.
disallowed_use: Do not copy product-facing result-page prose, report copy, UI copy, scoring presentation, repository data, or respondent-like samples into FermatMind assets.
Do not use repository popularity or implementation existence as psychometric validation evidence for FermatMind.

copy_policy.notes: Open-source GitHub material is implementation structure reference only for this pipeline, not a public copy source.

claim_trace_requirements: Any reference must preserve implementation-reference-only status and must not support reader-facing claims.

sources.title: FermatMind Big Five V2 formal result-page source authority
permitted_use: Use as citation authority for V2 module names, reading modes, interaction modules, and source-planning boundaries.
Use to trace future candidate scope back to internal V2 module authority.
disallowed_use: Do not wire the formal V2 document directly into runtime or frontend fallback.
Do not generalize draft body copy to all users without selector, route, editorial, safety, and rendered-preview QA.
Do not use this source to mark assets pilot-ready, runtime-ready, or production-ready.

copy_policy.notes: Internal V2 authority may guide module structure and claim scope; body copy requires a separate reviewed asset batch.

claim_trace_requirements: Every derived module claim must cite the source authority path and preserve not-runtime/staging-only boundaries.

sources.title: FermatMind Big Five longform O59 source trace
permitted_use: Use as citation trace for O59/C32/E20/A55/N68 staging-only source mapping.
Use to map O59 core-body sections to V2 modules and the eight-section result skeleton.
disallowed_use: Do not use O59 longform body as universal Big Five result copy.
Do not use this source for non-O59 canonical profiles without a separate source row and reviewed asset batch.
Do not expose private score tuples or internal combination keys in share-safe payloads.

copy_policy.notes: Internal longform source trace can support reviewed O59 staging candidates only; this ledger does not create copy.

claim_trace_requirements: Claims must include O59-only limitation and source-trace reference.
Share-safe usage requires share_safety_registry transformation and separate review.

sources.title: Existing Big Five Result Page V2 asset packs and master catalog
permitted_use: Use as current backend inventory citation for package count, readiness flags, and staged asset surfaces.
Use as input to gap audit and validator harness tasks.
disallowed_use: Do not treat existing package presence as proof that selector coverage, safety QA, editorial QA, route QA, or rendered-preview QA passed.
Do not import into CMS, production, or runtime based only on catalog presence.
Do not use staging selector candidates as final public payloads without validator and safety reports.

copy_policy.notes: Existing package metadata can be cited for inventory and gate state; body copy generation remains out of scope.

claim_trace_requirements: Inventory claims must cite a specific package path and readiness flags.
Claims about counts must be revalidated by the current task's local checks before promotion.

sources.title: Restricted BFI-2 item text, proprietary personality reports, and competitor result copy
permitted_use: Use only as a negative boundary for candidate generation, review, and safety scanning.
Use to reject copied item text, copied report prose, and copied competitor copy.
disallowed_use: Do not copy, paraphrase closely, summarize into substitute prose, embed, or transform BFI-2 item text.
Do not copy proprietary personality report prose, competitor result-page copy, or private sample payloads.
Do not use forbidden sources as source material for selector_asset or content_asset candidates.

copy_policy.notes: This row is a hard rejection boundary, not a usable source.

claim_trace_requirements: Any candidate traced to this source_label must be rejected and kept out of staging imports.

claim_trace_policy.public_payload_requirement: Only future candidates with an approved source row and explicit public-payload allowlist may enter review.
claim_trace_policy.share_payload_requirement: Shareable candidates require share_safety_registry transformation and no raw score, vector, percentile, private id, private URL, or internal metadata exposure.
claim_trace_policy.bfi_2_policy: BFI-2 is structure_reference_only. It may be used for high-level structure and terminology reference only; do not copy BFI-2 item text, scoring text, report copy, or body prose.

next_stage_blockers: No candidate generator exists in this PR.
This ledger does not validate generated selector_asset or content_asset drafts.
This ledger does not write content_assets staging packages.
This ledger does not mark any asset ready_for_pilot, ready_for_runtime, or ready_for_production.
